explanation: The requirement is: When the ventilator is in Start-up or VentilationOff mode, the in valve pressure shall be set to close and the out valve shall be open. Here, 'Start-up' corresponds to StartUpMode and 'VentilationOff' is StandbyMode in the atomic propositions. 'In valve pressure shall be set to close' is represented as inValveClose, and 'out valve shall be open' as outValveOpen. The structure 'When ... , ...' is translated as an implication. So: If (StartUpMode or StandbyMode) then (inValveClose and outValveOpen). Since this should hold globally (always), apply the G operator. Therefore, the final formula is: G((StartUpMode | StandbyMode) -> (inValveClose & outValveOpen)).

explanation: We need to specify that whenever (globally) the ventilator is in Start-up or Standby (VentilationOff) mode, both the in valve pressure must be set to close and the out valve must be open. The atomic propositions for these are: 'StartUpMode' for start-up, 'StandbyMode' for ventilation off, 'inValveClose' for in valve pressure set to close, and 'outValveOpen' for out valve open. 'When' at the start of the sentence means this is always required when the condition holds (globally: G). 'or' between modes becomes logical 'or'. The required result is the conjunction: inValveClose & outValveOpen. So, for every point in time (global/G), if (StartUpMode or StandbyMode) then (inValveClose and outValveOpen): G((StartUpMode | StandbyMode) -> (inValveClose & outValveOpen)).

We use (StartUpMode | StandbyMode) to represent 'in Start-up or VentilationOff mode'. The phrase 'whenever...' suggests a global (G) operator. 'the in valve pressure shall be set to close and the out valve shall be open' translates to (inValveClose & outValveOpen). This is expressed as an implication within the global operator: G((StartUpMode | StandbyMode) -> (inValveClose & outValveOpen)).

explanation:
First, identify the key modes: 'Start-up' and 'VentilationOff'. Start-up is represented by StartUpMode and VentilationOff by StandbyMode (from the definitions). The requirement says: whenever the system is in one of these modes, in valve pressure must be set to close (inValveClose is true) and out valve shall be open (outValveOpen is true). This is a global (G) constraint: at all times, if either StartUpMode or StandbyMode, then inValveClose and outValveOpen both hold. In LTL, 'or' is |, 'and' is &, and implication is ->. Thus, the formula is:
G((StartUpMode | StandbyMode) -> (inValveClose & outValveOpen)).
